channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: First Official Helldivers 2 Roadmap Promises New Biomes and More
item.content:encoded:
Arrowhead and Sony have announced the first official Helldivers 2 roadmap, and it's promising lots of new stuff for the shooter over the next few months, starting from this Friday.

In a post on Steam, Arrowhead reveals that the first major update on the roadmap, which will augment February's Machinery of Oppression drop, will land on March 20th (that's this coming Friday at time of writing).

The update, which is designated Update 6.1.0, will introduce new types of Illuminate enemies, as well as fleets that will field these enemies for you to do battle with. You can also look forward to new weather conditions caused by the update's Exostorm system, as well as a new mission type and lots of balancing changes.

The next update that will follow this is the aptly-named Update 6.2, which will arrive sometime in April. It'll bring new biomes and new galactic war campaigns for you to experience those biomes, plus a new enemy variant and more.

Another new biome and an accompanying set of galactic war campaigns will be introduced in June's Update 6.3, as well as a new Warbond. All of these updates will also bring lots of quality-of-life changes and balance improvements.

If you're unfamiliar with Helldivers 2, it's a multiplayer shooter that sees players descending onto alien planets in order to take down bugs, bots, and an intelligent alien faction known as the Illuminate.

Each session sees you and your squad launching onto a planet, taking down hordes of enemies, and completing objectives before extracting (assuming you're able to without getting overwhelmed by the bots and the bugs, of course).

Helldivers 2 is available right now for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S, and since it's getting a tranche of updates over the next few months, now is a good time to check it out if you haven't already. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
